mcqData.explanation: The Bangladesh Sericulture Board was established in 1977 to promote the silk industry.

mcqData.text: What kind of fabric is made from the fibers secreted by silkworms?
mcqData.explanation: Reshmi or Silk fabric is known for its luster, strength, and luxury.

mcqData.text: What is the name of the shell made of silk threads that the silkworm spins?
mcqData.explanation: The silkworm secretes liquid silk from its salivary glands to spin a protective Cocoon.

mcqData.text: Harmful to silkworms: (i) Uzi fly (ii) Red ant (iii) Spider
mcqData.explanation: Ants, spiders, and Uzi flies are common predators and parasites in sericulture.

mcqData.text: Stem: Kabir visited Rajshahi and saw silkworm larvae producing saliva from their mouths. Q: Which stage of silkworm rearing did Kabir see?
mcqData.explanation: Silkworms produce silk fluid (saliva) to spin a cocoon around themselves.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 161) Q: Care during this stage included: (i) Providing mulberry leaves (ii) Cleanliness (iii) Keeping ants away
mcqData.explanation: Hygiene and protection from predators like ants are crucial for healthy silkworm growth.

mcqData.text: Stem: Borokot is interested in sericulture. The officer said his region is the best for silk due to the climate. Q: Which district does Borokot live in?
mcqData.explanation: Rajshahi is known as the "Silk City" of Bangladesh due to its ideal environment for mulberry and silk.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Referring to silkworms) Q: Ideal conditions for silk larvae: (i) Temp $21-29^{\circ}C$ (ii) 70-80% Humidity (iii) Medium-aged mulberry leaves
mcqData.explanation: Controlled temperature and humidity are vital for the survival and productivity of silkworms.

mcqData.text: Which mushroom is cultivable all year round?
mcqData.explanation: Oyster mushrooms (Oyster/ঝিনুক) are highly adaptable to the climate of Bangladesh throughout the year.

mcqData.text: What is the seed of a mushroom called?
mcqData.explanation: Spawn consists of mushroom mycelium grown on a substrate like grain or sawdust.

mcqData.text: What temperature should be maintained in a mushroom cultivation room?
mcqData.explanation: Most common mushrooms like Oyster grow best between $20^{\circ}C$ to $30^{\circ}C$.

mcqData.text: Which pest attacks mushroom cultivation?
mcqData.explanation: Mushroom flies (Phorid/Sciarid flies) are common pests in mushroom houses.

mcqData.text: How to identify a good (edible) mushroom?
mcqData.explanation: Edible mushrooms look fresh and are typically grown in controlled environments.

mcqData.text: What is a mushroom?
mcqData.explanation: Mushrooms are the fruiting bodies of certain macro-fungi.

mcqData.text: Which mushroom can be grown all year?
mcqData.explanation: Oyster is the most reliable year-round variety in Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: Which is the best mushroom variety for cultivation in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Due to low cost and high yield, Oyster is considered the best for local farmers.

mcqData.text: Which mushroom is cultivated ONLY in winter?
mcqData.explanation: Button mushrooms require low temperatures ($15-22^{\circ}C$), making them winter-only in BD.

mcqData.text: Within how many days after placing packets in the room can mushrooms be harvested?
mcqData.explanation: Once pins appear, Oyster mushrooms are usually ready for harvest within 7-9 days.

mcqData.text: In which English letter shape should a mushroom spawn packet be cut?
mcqData.explanation: A 'D' shaped cut is the standard for opening mushroom packets to allow growth.

mcqData.text: Mushrooms grow through: (i) Spores (ii) Sexual reproduction (iii) Asexual reproduction
mcqData.explanation: Mushrooms primarily reproduce via spores and can have complex sexual cycles.

mcqData.text: For mushroom growth, it is necessary: (i) Oxygen (ii) Food (iii) Carbon dioxide
mcqData.explanation: Like humans, mushrooms take in Oxygen and release $CO_2$; they also need substrate as food.

mcqData.text: Mushrooms help cure: (i) Hypertension (ii) Anemia (iii) Hepatitis B
mcqData.explanation: Mushrooms are medicinal foods that help manage blood pressure, boost blood, and liver health.

mcqData.text: Mushrooms are cultivated in: (i) Winter (ii) Summer (iii) Monsoon
mcqData.explanation: Different varieties of mushrooms can be cultivated in all seasons in Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: Stem: Mishu cultivates mushrooms at home in winter. She considers specific factors for the room. Q: Which type of mushroom does Mishu cultivate?
mcqData.explanation: Since Mishu specifically grows them in winter, Button mushrooms are the most likely choice.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Referring to Mishu's mushroom room) Q: Factors for Mishu to consider: (i) Temp $20-30^{\circ}C$ (ii) Ventilation (iii) Bright direct sunlight
mcqData.explanation: Mushrooms need ventilation and specific temp, but direct sunlight is harmful; they prefer indirect light.

mcqData.text: Stem: Tamal sets up a room and collects 15 packets of spawn. Q: What was the ideal temperature in Tamal's mushroom room?
mcqData.explanation: Most mushrooms (like Oyster) thrive in the $20-25^{\circ}C$ (or up to $30^{\circ}C$) range.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 182) Q: How much mushroom can Tamal get from 15 packets?
mcqData.explanation: On average, one 200g spawn packet yields about 200-250g of mushroom. So 15 packets $\approx$ 3 kg.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Referring to a diagram of PDA/Tissue culture) Q: Which process can be cultivated by this method?
mcqData.explanation: Tissue culture and spawn production steps (PDA, Mother culture) are core to mushroom farming.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 184) Q: Characteristics: (i) Potato used for PDA (ii) Wheat used for Mother Culture (iii) Rice used for Pure Culture
mcqData.explanation: Potato Dextrose Agar (PDA) uses potatoes; Mother culture uses grains like wheat/sorghum.

mcqData.text: How many Queen bees are in a single bee colony?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) A colony has only one fertile Queen.

mcqData.text: What is the scientific rearing of bees called?
mcqData.explanation: Apiculture is the branch of agriculture focused on honeybees.

mcqData.text: Who is the most important bee in a colony for leadership?
mcqData.explanation: The Queen bee controls the hive's harmony through pheromones and ensures reproduction.

mcqData.text: When is the best time for honey production (in BD)?
mcqData.explanation: Spring (February-March) is the peak as mustard and litchi flowers are in full bloom.

mcqData.text: What percentage of a bee colony consists of Worker bees?
mcqData.explanation: About 90-95% of the bees in a hive are Workers.

mcqData.text: Wax glands are located on the abdomen of which bee?
mcqData.explanation: Only Worker bees have wax glands to build the honeycomb structure.

mcqData.text: What is a honeybee farm called?
mcqData.explanation: An 'Apiary' is a place where beehives of honeybees are kept.

mcqData.text: Which bee performs pollination in plants?
mcqData.explanation: Worker bees forage for nectar and pollen, accidentally performing pollination.

mcqData.text: Who is the 'Drone' in a honeybee colony?
mcqData.explanation: Drones are the fertile male bees of the colony.

mcqData.text: From which year did commercial bee-keeping start in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Commercial apiculture gained momentum in 1977 with the help of various NGOs and BSCIC.

mcqData.text: Honey from which flower is considered the best quality?
mcqData.explanation: Litchi honey is highly prized for its taste, color, and aroma.

mcqData.text: The presence of which pest is harmful near the beehive?
mcqData.explanation: Ants can invade the hive to steal honey and attack larvae.

mcqData.text: Which bee species is most famous for commercial honey farming?
mcqData.explanation: Apis mellifera (European bee) is the global favorite for commercial production.

mcqData.text: How many Queen bees are needed to lead a colony?
mcqData.explanation: (Same as Q. 186) Leadership is strictly held by one Queen.

mcqData.text: How many types of bees are there in a beehive?
mcqData.explanation: There are 3 types: Queen, Drone (Male), and Worker.

mcqData.text: What is produced from fertilized eggs? (i) Worker bee (ii) Drone/Male bee (iii) Queen bee
mcqData.explanation: Fertilized eggs produce females (Queen/Worker), while unfertilized eggs produce Drones.

mcqData.text: From honey, we get: (i) Carbohydrates (ii) Vitamins (iii) Minerals
mcqData.explanation: Honey is a complete food containing sugars (glucose/fructose), various vitamins, and minerals.

mcqData.text: Basic components of honey production: (i) Nectar (ii) Pollen (iii) Flower
mcqData.explanation: Bees collect nectar (juice) and pollen from flowers to produce honey and food.

mcqData.text: Stem: A beneficial social insect and one of man's best friends. Q: What is the name of this insect?
mcqData.explanation: Honeybees are social insects that live in colonies and provide honey and pollination.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 204) Q: Through this insect: (i) Employment is created (ii) Wax and honey are obtained (iii) Crop production decreases
mcqData.explanation: Bees increase crop production through pollination, they do not decrease it.

mcqData.text: What is the main objective of Artificial Insemination?
mcqData.explanation: The primary goal is to improve the genetic quality of the offspring using superior bull semen.

mcqData.text: What is the ideal age of a bull for artificial insemination (semen collection)?
mcqData.explanation: Bulls aged 2-3 years are typically mature enough for high-quality semen collection.

mcqData.text: Climate refers to the average atmospheric condition of how many years?
mcqData.explanation: Climate is the long-term average (usually 20-30 years) of weather patterns in a region.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Referring to a seasonal chart/diagram) Q: Crops belonging to the 'A' season (Kharif-1/Summer): (i) Arum (ii) Jute (iii) Mustard
mcqData.explanation: Arum (Kocu) and Jute are summer crops; Mustard is a winter (Rabi) crop.

mcqData.text: If air humidity is high, what effect can it have on crop production?
mcqData.explanation: High humidity creates a moist environment ideal for the growth of fungi and bacteria.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Long-Day plant?
mcqData.explanation: Long-day plants (like Cabbage, Spinach) require more than 12 hours of light to flower.

mcqData.text: Stem: Continuous fog in Rangpur slowed Milton's potato growth. Q: What weather role affected the slow growth? (i) Temperature (ii) Light (iii) Rainfall
mcqData.explanation: Fog blocks sunlight and lowers temperature, both of which are essential for photosynthesis.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Short-Day crop?
mcqData.explanation: Cauliflower and most winter crops are short-day plants, needing less light to flower.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Climate refers to the average of how many years?
mcqData.explanation: Consistent with international standards, 25-30 years is the benchmark for climate.

mcqData.text: How many layers are there in the atmosphere?
mcqData.explanation: The 5 layers are: Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere, and Exosphere.

mcqData.text: Which one influences the selection of crop types and varieties in a region?
mcqData.explanation: Climate determines what can grow in a region over the long term (e.g., tropical vs temperate).

mcqData.text: In which region is the temperature generally highest?
mcqData.explanation: The Sun's rays fall vertically on the Equatorial region year-round, keeping it hot.

mcqData.text: What is the type of climate in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Bangladesh has a moderate (সমভাবাপন্ন) climate, meaning it's neither too hot nor too cold.

mcqData.text: What is the cause of increasing greenhouse gases in the atmosphere?
mcqData.explanation: Deforestation reduces the absorption of $CO_2$, leading to an increase in greenhouse gases.

mcqData.text: What is the daily condition of the atmosphere in a place called?
mcqData.explanation: Weather (আবহাওয়া) describes short-term changes; Climate is the long-term average.

mcqData.text: Which plants grow in regions with high temperature and heavy rainfall?
mcqData.explanation: Rain forests or evergreen forests (চিরহরিৎ) thrive in hot and humid tropical zones.

mcqData.text: Into how many (climate) zones has the Earth been divided?
mcqData.explanation: The 4 main zones are: Tropical, Subtropical, Temperate, and Polar.

mcqData.text: Which of the following controls crop yield and production?
mcqData.explanation: While weather affects a single harvest, Climate dictates overall productivity.

mcqData.text: Which crop grows well in the Equatorial region?
mcqData.explanation: Jute requires high heat and moisture, which is abundant in equatorial/tropical zones.

mcqData.text: What is the percentage of Oxygen in the atmosphere?
mcqData.explanation: Nitrogen is about 78%, and Oxygen is approximately 20.99% or 21%.

mcqData.text: To describe climate, weather average of how many years is taken?
mcqData.explanation: 30 years is the most scientifically accepted duration for climate assessment.

mcqData.text: What is the weather like in places near the sea?
mcqData.explanation: The sea acts as a temperature stabilizer, keeping the coastal weather moderate.

mcqData.text: How many agro-meteorological stations are there in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: There are 12 specialized agro-met stations that provide weather data for farming.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of Climate?
mcqData.explanation: Climate changes over decades or centuries, unlike weather which changes hourly.

mcqData.text: Which of the following influences the daily care/management of crops?
mcqData.explanation: Farmers decide on irrigation or spraying based on the daily weather forecast.

mcqData.text: Climate is the average of weather over how many years?
mcqData.explanation: Consistent with global meteorological standards for defining a region's climate.

mcqData.text: What is the reason for the variation in year-round rainfall in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Bangladesh's proximity to the Bay of Bengal and the Himalayas dictates its rainfall.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Effect of high air humidity on crop production?
mcqData.explanation: High humidity prevents sweat/water from evaporating, favoring fungal growth.

mcqData.text: What is the cause of the variation in day-length?
mcqData.explanation: The Earth's tilted axis and its orbit around the sun cause seasonal changes in day length.

mcqData.text: Which has the most significant impact on agriculture?
mcqData.explanation: Rainfall is the primary factor determining the cropping pattern in Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: Weather has the most impact on which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: Clouds and rain (weather) directly determine how many hours of sun a crop gets.

mcqData.text: What is the main cause of variation in air temperature?
mcqData.explanation: Longer days mean more exposure to solar radiation, leading to higher temperatures.

mcqData.text: The amount of light depends on which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: The duration of the 'day' (photoperiod) dictates the total light energy available.

mcqData.text: Where is the highest rainfall recorded in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Lalkhan (Sylhet region) is known for the highest rainfall; Lalpur (Natore) is the driest.

mcqData.text: What is the ideal temperature for growing winter vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: Winter crops like cauliflower or cabbage prefer a cool $15-20^{\circ}C$ range.

mcqData.text: Which plays the biggest role in the overall growth of a crop?
mcqData.explanation: Temperature controls the rate of physiological processes like germination and growth.

mcqData.text: What is the name of the instrument used to measure air humidity?
mcqData.explanation: A Hygrometer measures the amount of water vapor (humidity) in the air.

mcqData.text: What is the average annual rainfall in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Bangladesh receives an average of 225-230 cm of rain annually.

mcqData.text: The force exerted by air on the Earth's surface is called—
mcqData.explanation: Air pressure (বায়ুর চাপ) is measured using a Barometer.

mcqData.text: What is the best temperature for jute cultivation?
mcqData.explanation: Jute is a tropical crop that thrives in high heat ($90^{\circ}F \approx 32^{\circ}C$).

mcqData.text: In which months does Bangladesh receive the most rainfall?
mcqData.explanation: The peak monsoon months of July and August see the highest precipitation.

mcqData.text: When does plant transpiration decrease?
mcqData.explanation: When air is already saturated with water vapor (high humidity), plants cannot release more water.

mcqData.text: What happens when relative humidity is low?
mcqData.explanation: Low humidity means the air is dry, which accelerates the process of evaporation.

mcqData.text: What is the name of the instrument used to measure rainfall?
mcqData.explanation: A Rain Gauge (রেইনগেজ) measures the amount of precipitation in millimeters.

mcqData.text: How does an increase in temperature affect potato production?
mcqData.explanation: Potatoes need cool temperatures ($15-20^{\circ}C$) to form tubers; high heat reduces yield.

mcqData.text: Which plant grows well in regions with very low rainfall?
mcqData.explanation: Cacti are xerophytes, specifically adapted to store water and survive in arid deserts.

mcqData.text: Relative humidity affects: (i) Maturity stage (ii) Disease infection (iii) Grain filling stage
mcqData.explanation: Humidity influences how seeds dry, how pathogens spread, and how nutrients move in the plant.

mcqData.text: What is the duration from sunrise to sunset called?
mcqData.explanation: Day-length (দিবা-দৈর্ঘ্য) is the photoperiod that dictates flowering in many plants.

mcqData.text: Which difference causes the main variation between weather and climate?
mcqData.explanation: While rainfall/wind change daily, day-length follows a long-term predictable solar pattern.

mcqData.text: What is the typical day-length in Bangladesh during winter?
mcqData.explanation: Winter days are shorter in the Northern Hemisphere, usually around 10 to 11 hours.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Short-Day plant?
mcqData.explanation: Short-day plants (like Aman rice or pulses) require less than 12 hours of light to flower.

mcqData.text: Which of the following plants flowers during Long-Day conditions?
mcqData.explanation: Onions and many summer crops are long-day plants, requiring more than 12 hours of light.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Day-Neutral plant?
mcqData.explanation: Day-neutral plants like Sunflowers flower regardless of the duration of daylight.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Summer (Kharif) season crop?
mcqData.explanation: Soybean is primarily grown in the Kharif season; others are Rabi (winter) crops.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Winter (Rabi) crop?
mcqData.explanation: Chickpeas require the cool, dry climate of the winter season.

mcqData.text: What is the maximum temperature during summer in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Summer peaks usually stay within the $28^{\circ}-42^{\circ}C$ range, though it can vary.

mcqData.text: What is the minimum temperature during summer in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Summer nights in Bangladesh typically don't drop below $22^{\circ}-30^{\circ}C$.

mcqData.text: In which months does the country experience its highest temperatures?
mcqData.explanation: The period from March to September covers the hottest months (Summer and Monsoon).

mcqData.text: What is the maximum temperature during winter in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Winter days are pleasant, with maximums usually between $24^{\circ}$ and $28^{\circ}C$.

mcqData.text: What is the minimum temperature during winter in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Winter nights can get chilly, dropping to $7^{\circ}-15^{\circ}C$ on average.

mcqData.text: When do Flash Floods (ঢল বন্যা) typically occur?
mcqData.explanation: Flash floods in the Haor regions usually occur during the pre-monsoon (March-April).

mcqData.text: In which months do early flash floods typically occur?
mcqData.explanation: (Similar to Q. 60) Pre-monsoon flash floods are common in haor areas during March-April.

mcqData.text: At what speed (miles per hour) do raindrops hit the ground?
mcqData.explanation: The terminal velocity of a typical raindrop is approximately 24-25 miles per hour.

mcqData.text: What is the average annual rainfall in Lalpur, Rajshahi?
mcqData.explanation: Lalpur (Natore/Rajshahi region) is the driest place in BD, receiving about 125 cm rain.

mcqData.text: What is the relative humidity level during fog?
mcqData.explanation: Fog occurs when air is fully saturated with water vapor, reaching 100% humidity.

mcqData.text: Boro rice, mustard, and radish are crops of which season?
mcqData.explanation: These are winter (Rabi) crops grown between October and March.

mcqData.text: Flowering is delayed if the night temperature is—
mcqData.explanation: Cold stress (below $10^{\circ}C$) slows down the physiological processes required for flowering.

mcqData.text: Field crops are most affected by which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: Rainfall dictates the irrigation needs, sowing time, and overall yield of field crops.

mcqData.text: What is the optimum temperature for rice cultivation?
mcqData.explanation: Rice thrives best in a warm climate with an average temperature of $25-30^{\circ}C$.

mcqData.text: In which months can Jujube (Kul) budding be done in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Budding of Jujube is most successful during the humid months of July and August.

mcqData.text: What is the optimum temperature for wheat germination?
mcqData.explanation: Wheat seeds germinate best when the soil temperature is around $20-25^{\circ}C$ ($22.5^{\circ}C$).

mcqData.text: Which is a characteristic of the Rabi season?
mcqData.explanation: Rabi is the winter season, characterized by low temperatures and dry air.

mcqData.text: Stem: Masud grows Eggplant, Chili, and Papaya. Q: When are these crops grown?
mcqData.explanation: These crops are "Day-neutral" or adaptable to both winter and summer in Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 72) Q: Characteristics: (i) Grow in high/low temp (ii) Grow in high/low humidity (iii) Long-day crops
mcqData.explanation: These versatile crops handle varied climates but are generally day-neutral, not long-day.

mcqData.text: Stem: Anis grew Potato in one season and Jute in another. Q: Anis's first crop (Potato) belongs to which season?
mcqData.explanation: Potato is a classic Rabi (winter) crop requiring cool weather.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 74) Q: Second crop (Jute) season characteristics: (i) High rainfall (ii) Hailstorms (iii) Flash floods
mcqData.explanation: Jute is a Kharif-1 crop; this season is marked by nor'westers, hail, and high rain.

mcqData.text: What is the general temperature suitable for seed germination?
mcqData.explanation: Most seeds germinate optimally at a warm room temperature of around $25^{\circ}C$.

mcqData.text: If air humidity is high, transpiration—
mcqData.explanation: High humidity reduces the vapor pressure gradient, slowing down water loss from leaves.

mcqData.text: In which months do hailstorms (শিলাবৃষ্টি) usually occur in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: Hailstorms are common during the 'Kalbaishakhi' (Nor'wester) season in March-April.

mcqData.text: At what temperature do most crops grow well?
mcqData.explanation: The $23-29^{\circ}C$ range is ideal for the metabolic activities of most tropical crops.

mcqData.text: For convenience of crop production, the year is divided into how many seasons?
mcqData.explanation: In Bangladesh agriculture, the year is divided into two main seasons: Rabi and Kharif.

mcqData.text: What is the duration of the Rabi season?
mcqData.explanation: Rabi season officially starts from mid-October/November and ends in February.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Rabi crop?
mcqData.explanation: Tobacco is grown during the dry winter months (Rabi season) in Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: What is the duration of the Kharif-1 season?
mcqData.explanation: Kharif-1 is the summer season, usually spanning from March to June.

mcqData.text: Which is a Kharif-1 season crop?
mcqData.explanation: Jute is the major summer crop, requiring heat and early rain to grow.

mcqData.text: What is the duration of the Kharif-2 season?
mcqData.explanation: Kharif-2 is the monsoon season, lasting from July to October.

mcqData.text: Which is a Kharif-2 season crop?
mcqData.explanation: Aman rice is the characteristic monsoon crop of the Kharif-2 season.

mcqData.text: How many types of Monsoon winds flow in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: There are two main monsoon flows: South-West (Summer) and North-East (Winter).

mcqData.text: From which direction does the monsoon wind flow during summer?
mcqData.explanation: The South-West monsoon brings moisture from the Bay of Bengal, causing rain.

mcqData.text: In which direction does the North-West/North-East monsoon flow?
mcqData.explanation: Winter monsoon winds are dry because they flow from the cold landmass toward the sea.

mcqData.text: What happens due to the influence of Monsoon winds?
mcqData.explanation: Monsoon is synonymous with the rainy season in Bangladesh and South Asia.

mcqData.text: In summer, rain occurs due to the influence of which monsoon wind?
mcqData.explanation: (Same as Q. 88) This wind is responsible for the majority of BD's annual rainfall.

mcqData.text: Why do many crops grow in this country during summer?
mcqData.explanation: The monsoon climate provides both the heat and water necessary for rapid crop growth.

mcqData.text: Due to the influence of monsoon climate: (i) Crop production increases (ii) Silt deposits in rivers (iii) Fisheries are harmed
mcqData.explanation: Monsoon floods bring fertile silt, helping crops, though extreme floods can damage fish farms.

mcqData.text: Which country is a classic example of a Monsoon climate country?
mcqData.explanation: Bangladesh's entire economy and agriculture are traditionally dictated by the monsoon.

mcqData.text: In which season does the monsoon wind flow from the North-East?
mcqData.explanation: During winter, cold winds come from the North-East (Himalayan direction).

mcqData.text: From which direction does the humid (moist) monsoon wind flow?
mcqData.explanation: Humidity comes from the Bay of Bengal, located to the South-West of Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: What is the nature of the climate in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Bangladesh's climate is temperate/moderate (সমভাবাপন্ন).

mcqData.text: Summer rain is caused by which wind?
mcqData.explanation: The South-West monsoon is the lifeline of summer agriculture.

mcqData.text: From which direction does the wind blow in winter?
mcqData.explanation: Cold and dry winds come from the North and North-West during the Rabi season.

mcqData.text: How many types of Monsoon winds are there?
mcqData.explanation: The seasonal reversal of winds happens twice a year in this region.

mcqData.text: Kalbaishakhi (Nor'wester) occurs due to which monsoon wind?
mcqData.explanation: The North-West wind brings the violent thunderstorms known as Kalbaishakhi in spring.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Which of the following is a Long-Day plant?
mcqData.explanation: Cabbage requires longer periods of daylight (over 12 hours) to initiate flowering.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Which of the following is a Short-Day crop?
mcqData.explanation: Cauliflower is a classic winter crop that flowers when the days are shorter.

mcqData.text: Dahlia and Chrysanthemum are what kind of plants?
mcqData.explanation: These flowers bloom in winter when the daylight duration is short.

mcqData.text: Chrysanthemum (চন্দ্রমল্লিকা) is a flower of which day-length?
mcqData.explanation: (Same as Q. 104) It is one of the most prominent winter (short-day) flowers.

mcqData.text: Long-Day plants need more than how many hours of light?
mcqData.explanation: Long-day plants generally require a photoperiod of more than 12 hours to flower.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Day-neutral plant?
mcqData.explanation: Sunflowers are not significantly affected by the length of the day for flowering.

mcqData.text: Which is a light-sensitive (Photosensitive) rice variety?
mcqData.explanation: BR-22 (Kiron) and BR-23 (Dishari) are famous photosensitive Aman varieties.

mcqData.text: What kind of day-length does tomato need for flowering and fruiting?
mcqData.explanation: Tomato is a day-neutral plant; it can produce flowers and fruit in various day lengths.

mcqData.text: What percentage of yield can decrease due to drought?
mcqData.explanation: Depending on the severity, drought can cause massive losses ranging from 15% to 90%.

mcqData.text: Which is a drought-tolerant crop?
mcqData.explanation: Sesame has a deep root system and can survive in very low moisture conditions.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a drought-tolerant rice variety?
mcqData.explanation: BR-21 (Niamat) is specifically developed for drought-prone upland areas.

mcqData.text: Which is a light-insensitive (Photo-insensitive) rice variety?
mcqData.explanation: BRRI Dhan-43 is an Aus variety that is not sensitive to the length of the day.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Long-day plants need more than how many hours of light?
mcqData.explanation: The threshold for long-day plants is typically 12 to 14 hours of light.

mcqData.text: For which plant is the 'dark period' particularly important?
mcqData.explanation: Short-day plants like Soybean actually require a continuous long 'dark' period to flower.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT a short-day plant?
mcqData.explanation: Tomato is day-neutral, meaning it doesn't fall into the short or long-day category.

mcqData.text: Who first provided the concept of Photoperiodism (আলোক সংবেদনশীলতা)?
mcqData.explanation: W.W. Garner and H.A. Allard discovered photoperiodism in 1920.

mcqData.text: Into how many groups can plants be divided based on light sensitivity?
mcqData.explanation: The 3 groups are: Short-Day, Long-Day, and Day-Neutral plants.

mcqData.text: What are plants called that are not affected by the length of day or night?
mcqData.explanation: Day-neutral plants (আলোক নিরপেক্ষ) flower regardless of the duration of light they receive.

mcqData.text: Based on day-length, crops can be divided into how many groups?
mcqData.explanation: The three groups are: Short-Day, Long-Day, and Day-Neutral plants.

mcqData.text: Short-day plants require less than how many hours of light for fruiting?
mcqData.explanation: Short-day plants typically need a photoperiod of less than 12 hours to flower.

mcqData.text: All varieties of which of the following crops are Short-Day plants?
mcqData.explanation: Jute is inherently a short-day plant; it flowers quickly when days shorten.

mcqData.text: What is the typical light duration for Long-Day plants?
mcqData.explanation: Long-day plants thrive when they receive 14 to 18 hours of light per day.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT a Long-Day plant?
mcqData.explanation: Jute is a short-day plant, whereas Aus rice can handle longer light periods.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Day-Neutral (Light-insensitive) plant?
mcqData.explanation: Maize (ভুট্টা) is widely known for being insensitive to photoperiod changes.

mcqData.text: Which of the following grows well in the shade?
mcqData.explanation: Betel leaf is a shade-loving plant (Sciophyte) that requires low light intensity.

mcqData.text: How many types of light-sensitive plants are there?
mcqData.explanation: Broadly, they are categorized into two: Light-sensitive and Light-insensitive.

mcqData.text: What percentage of the light it receives does a tree use for photosynthesis?
mcqData.explanation: Remarkably, plants only utilize about 1-2% of total solar energy for photosynthesis.

mcqData.text: What is the optimum temperature for rice plants?
mcqData.explanation: Rice is a tropical/subtropical crop that grows best between $25^{\circ}$ and $35^{\circ}C$.

mcqData.text: What is the minimum temperature for the adaptation of rice plants?
mcqData.explanation: Below $10^{\circ}C$, rice plants suffer from cold stress and growth stops.

mcqData.text: What is the maximum temperature for the adaptation of rice?
mcqData.explanation: Beyond $40^{\circ}C$, the physiological functions of rice plants are severely damaged.

5.chapterName: Chapter 5 : Field and Horticultural Crop Production

mcqData.text: What is the main reason for ginning in cotton?
mcqData.explanation: Ginning is the mechanical process of separating cotton fibers from their seeds.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a harmful pest for cotton?
mcqData.explanation: Bollworms are the most destructive pests for cotton bolls (the fruit of the cotton plant).

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a variety of Guava?
mcqData.explanation: Swarupkathi is a famous local variety of guava in Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: What is a characteristic of Sugarcane (a sugar crop)?
mcqData.explanation: Ratoon cropping means growing a second crop from the stubble left after the first harvest.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Image of a Stem Borer moth/larva) Q: What is the name of the rice pest shown in the image?
mcqData.explanation: Stem Borer (মাজরা পোকা) larvae tunnel into the stem, causing 'dead heart' or 'white head'.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. above) Q: The pest shown: (i) Is a type of moth (ii) Larvae cut the stem from inside (iii) Attacks during milk stage
mcqData.explanation: Gandhi bugs attack during the milk stage; Stem Borers attack the stem during growth.

mcqData.text: What is the main reason for pruning (অঙ্গ ছাটাই) fruit trees?
mcqData.explanation: Pruning removes dead or excess branches to allow light and boost fruit production.

mcqData.text: What is a characteristic of fiber crops (e.g., Jute)?
mcqData.explanation: Many fiber crops or related industrial crops can be grown from cuttings or vegetative parts.

mcqData.text: What is the cause of Blast disease in rice?
mcqData.explanation: Rice Blast is caused by the fungus Magnaporthe oryzae.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a harmful pest of Sugarcane?
mcqData.explanation: Termites are a major threat to sugarcane sets and growing stalks.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a variety of Orange (Mandarin)?
mcqData.explanation: Nagpuri is a very popular variety of orange/mandarin in South Asia.

mcqData.text: Why is Urea ($CO(NH_2)_2$) applied in three installments in rice cultivation?
mcqData.explanation: Applying urea in splits ensures the plant can absorb it efficiently without it leaching away.

mcqData.text: Stem: Latif grew ratoon sugarcane. The leaves turned yellow and the stem interior was pale red. Q: What is the cause of this disease?
mcqData.explanation: This is "Red Rot" (লাল পচা রোগ) of sugarcane, which is caused by a fungus.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a spice crop?
mcqData.explanation: Onion, garlic, and ginger are classified as spice crops in agriculture.

mcqData.text: Stem: Farmer Rafiq can't rot jute due to water shortage. The officer suggests a special method. Q: What is the name of this method?
mcqData.explanation: Ribbon retting involves stripping the bark and rotting it in small tanks with less water.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. above) Q: In this jute retting method: (i) Fiber quality is better (ii) Jute stick stays hard (iii) Needs more space & water
mcqData.explanation: Ribbon retting improves quality and saves water, though the jute sticks (patkhori) are not preserved whole.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Image of a Pamri Beetle/Hispa) Q: Which crop does the pest in the image attack?
mcqData.explanation: The Rice Hispa (পামরি পোকা) is a major pest that damages rice leaves.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. above) Q: Symptoms of this pest attack: (i) Leaves look burnt (ii) Larvae mine inside leaves (iii) Adults scrape leaf surface
mcqData.explanation: Pamri beetle adults scrape the green part of the leaf, leaving white parallel streaks.

mcqData.text: Ginning is performed on which crop?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Ginning is specific to the cotton industry for seed-fiber separation.

mcqData.text: What is the most dangerous disease of Sugarcane?
mcqData.explanation: Red Rot (লাল পচা) is the most devastating disease for sugarcane farmers.

mcqData.text: Which of the following can be eaten both as a vegetable and in a salad?
mcqData.explanation: Onions are widely used as a basic vegetable in cooking and raw in salads.

mcqData.text: Which of the following are shade-loving plants? (i) Cotton (ii) Tea (iii) Ginger
mcqData.explanation: Tea and Ginger thrive in partial shade, while cotton requires full sunlight.

mcqData.text: In which type of soil does orange grow best?
mcqData.explanation: Oranges and other citrus fruits prefer slightly acidic soil for optimal growth.

mcqData.text: What is the primary medium for guava tree propagation?
mcqData.explanation: Air layering or 'Guti Kolom' is the most common and successful method for guava.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) What is the cause of Red Rot disease in sugarcane?
mcqData.explanation: Red Rot is caused by the fungus Colletotrichum falcatum.

mcqData.text: Which rice disease is caused by a virus infection?
mcqData.explanation: Tungro is a viral disease spread by the Green Leafhopper.

mcqData.text: What is the most advanced method of sugarcane cultivation?
mcqData.explanation: STP (Space Transplanting) is considered an advanced and efficient method.

mcqData.text: Stem: Last year, black spots appeared on Guavas in Amalesh's garden, reducing market value. Q: Which disease affected the guavas?
mcqData.explanation: Anthracnose causes dark, sunken spots on fruits and leaves.

mcqData.text: Stem: Manik's rice plants have stunted growth and fewer tillers. Pale green/yellow lines appear along the veins. Q: Which disease is this?
mcqData.explanation: These are classic symptoms of the Tungro virus in rice.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. above) Q: What steps can Manik take to remedy this? (i) Use light traps (ii) Uproot infected plants (iii) Use approved pesticides
mcqData.explanation: Light traps catch the carrier insects; uprooting stops the spread; pesticides kill the hoppers.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a variety of Onion?
mcqData.explanation: Taherpuri is a very popular high-yielding local variety of onion.

mcqData.text: What is the importance of oilseed crops?
mcqData.explanation: Vegetable oils are essential raw materials for soaps, cosmetics, and margarine.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Aman season rice variety?
mcqData.explanation: BRRI Dhan-33 is a popular short-duration Aman rice variety.

mcqData.text: Fruits rich in Vitamin C: (i) Orange (ii) Guava (iii) Jujube (Kul)
mcqData.explanation: All three fruits are excellent sources of Vitamin C and antioxidants.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a crop with medicinal properties?
mcqData.explanation: Garlic is well-known for its antibiotic and heart-health benefits.

mcqData.text: What is the food for silkworms?
mcqData.explanation: Silkworms (Bombyx mori) feed exclusively on mulberry leaves to produce silk.

mcqData.text: Which family do cereal (grain) crops belong to?
mcqData.explanation: All major cereals like rice, wheat, and maize belong to the Gramineae family.

mcqData.text: What percentage of fiber in the textile sector comes from cotton?
mcqData.explanation: Cotton is the leading natural fiber, providing about 70-75% of global textile fiber.

mcqData.text: Which fruit is beneficial for common cold and fever?
mcqData.explanation: Oranges are rich in Vitamin C, which helps boost the immune system against colds.

mcqData.text: Stem: Sadeq Mia is a high-quality sugarcane farmer. He profited from Ratoon (Muri) sugarcane. Q: Which variety did he likely plant?
mcqData.explanation: Ishwardi-16 is a popular variety well-suited for Ratoon (Muri) sugarcane cultivation.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. above) Q: Reason for Ratoon sugarcane: (i) Lower cost (ii) Less time (iii) More profit
mcqData.explanation: Ratoon farming saves on land prep and seed costs, leading to higher profit in less time.

mcqData.text: How long does it take for a full moth to emerge from a silkworm egg?
mcqData.explanation: The incubation period for silkworm eggs is typically 9 to 14 days.

mcqData.text: What causes Blast disease in rice?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Rice Blast is a fungal disease that can destroy an entire harvest.

mcqData.text: Examples of Horticultural (Garden) crops: (i) Potato, Brinjal (ii) Onion, Ginger (iii) Gram, Rice, Jute
mcqData.explanation: Vegetables and spices are garden crops; rice, jute, and pulses are field crops.

mcqData.text: Critical stages for irrigation in rice: (i) Initial tillering (ii) Flowering (iii) Rooting stage
mcqData.explanation: Water is essential during tillering, panicle initiation, and flowering for high yields.

mcqData.text: What is the cultivation of silk called?
mcqData.explanation: Sericulture is the rearing of silkworms for the production of silk.

mcqData.text: What is Trichoderma?
mcqData.explanation: Trichoderma is a beneficial fungus used as a bio-pesticide to control soil-borne diseases.

mcqData.text: In the SRI method, how old should the rice seedlings be for transplanting?
mcqData.explanation: System of Rice Intensification (SRI) uses very young seedlings, usually 10-15 days old.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a cereal (grain) crop?
mcqData.explanation: Kaon is a minor cereal, while sunflower is an oilseed and the others are fiber crops.

mcqData.text: Stem: Manik's rice plants have stunted growth and fewer tillers. Pale green/yellow lines appear along the veins. Q: Remedy steps: (i) Light traps (ii) Uproot plants (iii) Use pesticides
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Comprehensive control involves removing hosts and killing the vector.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Image of Budding/Grafting method) Q: Which crop is this method applicable for?
mcqData.explanation: Budding or grafting is a standard method for propagating fruit trees like Orange.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Image of Budding/Grafting method) Q: Reason for this method: (i) Higher yield (ii) Disease control (iii) Pest control
mcqData.explanation: Vegetative propagation ensures high yield and preserves parental qualities, not direct pest control.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Image of a Cotton boll/plant) Q: What type of crop is shown?
mcqData.explanation: Cotton is the most important natural fiber crop in the world.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Image of a Cotton boll/plant) Q: Harmful pests of this crop: (i) Aphid (ii) Bollworm (iii) Red Gandhi
mcqData.explanation: Aphids and Bollworms are major cotton pests; Red Gandhi bugs are rice pests.

6.chapterName: Chapter 6 : Processing and Preservation of Fruits and Vegetables

mcqData.text: Which method is indicated by 'A' in the given chart?
mcqData.explanation: Based on standard charts, 'A' usually refers to solar/artificial drying (shukie shongrokkhon).

mcqData.text: Which fruits can be preserved using the method marked '?' (Drying)? (i) Dates (ii) Grapes (iii) Jujube
mcqData.explanation: Dates, raisins (grapes), and jujubes are commonly preserved by sun-drying.

mcqData.text: What is meant by 'Curing'?
mcqData.explanation: Curing involves drying the outer skin of tubers (like onions/potatoes) to heal bruises.

mcqData.text: Which acid is used in making Jelly?
mcqData.explanation: Citric acid provides the necessary acidity for pectin to set into a jelly.

mcqData.text: Vegetables are treated/sterilized using which rays?
mcqData.explanation: Gamma rays are used for food irradiation to kill microorganisms and extend shelf life.

mcqData.text: Which gas is used to treat/preserve vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: Sulfur dioxide ($SO_2$) acts as a preservative and prevents browning in fruits/veg.

mcqData.text: Which hormone controls plant growth and fruit ripening?
mcqData.explanation: Ethylene is the natural gas/hormone responsible for the ripening of fruits.

mcqData.text: What is dipping vegetables in hot water before preservation called?
mcqData.explanation: Blanching stops enzyme activity and preserves color and texture before freezing.

mcqData.text: Which acid is used in making Pickles?
mcqData.explanation: Acetic acid (Vinegar) is the primary preservative used in pickling.

mcqData.text: Which fruit can be preserved by dehydration?
mcqData.explanation: Dehydrated grapes become raisins, a globally popular preserved fruit.

mcqData.text: Which gas is emitted most during fruit ripening?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Ethylene emission peaks during the 'climacteric' phase of ripening.

mcqData.text: Kanchannagar, Mukundapuri, Swarupkathi are varieties of which fruit?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) These are all popular high-yielding varieties of Guava in BD.

mcqData.text: In what state are fruits preserved using the wax coating method?
mcqData.explanation: Edible wax coating is applied to fresh fruits to reduce moisture loss and respiration.

mcqData.text: Stem: Shamoly wants to save raw mangoes fallen in a storm. Q: What can she make?
mcqData.explanation: Raw mangoes are excellent for making Murabba (sweet preserve) or pickles.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 14) Q: Preservation methods for pickles: (i) Extra salt (ii) Mustard oil (iii) Temp control
mcqData.explanation: Pickling primarily relies on salt, oil, and acid (vinegar), not cold storage.

mcqData.text: What is the main cause of vegetable spoilage?
mcqData.explanation: Bacteria, fungi, and yeasts are the primary biological agents of spoilage.

mcqData.text: Which 'seed' (tuber) is stored in cold storage (hima-gar)?
mcqData.explanation: Potatoes are stored on a large scale in cold storages to maintain supply year-round.

mcqData.text: Marketing problems for fruit/veg in BD: (i) Transport (ii) Traffic jam (iii) Extortion
mcqData.explanation: These are all structural/social hurdles in the agricultural supply chain.

mcqData.text: What has the most influence on fruit spoilage in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: High tropical temperature and humidity accelerate the decay process.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is used to make French Fries?
mcqData.explanation: Potatoes with high starch content are ideal for making French fries.

mcqData.text: What is the main reason for preserving fruits and vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: The primary goal of preservation is to stop the decay caused by microbes and enzymes.

mcqData.text: Stem: Zubaida preserves fruits using a dense sugar solution (min. 60% concentration). Q: What type of preservation method is this?
mcqData.explanation: Preserving in syrup (like Murabba) is a form of processing that inhibits bacterial growth.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 22) Q: Which fruits can be preserved using this method? (i) Mango (ii) Guava (iii) Pineapple
mcqData.explanation: Mango, Guava, Pineapple, and Amla are commonly preserved in sugar syrup.

mcqData.text: Stem: Rashid grew potatoes; to get a better price later, he decided on long-term storage. Q: Which method did he use?
mcqData.explanation: Cold storage is the standard industrial method for preserving potatoes for months.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 24) Q: Due to Rashid's decision: (i) Crop is saved from rot (ii) Year-round demand met (iii) Fair price obtained
mcqData.explanation: Storage stabilizes the market, prevents waste, and ensures profit for the farmer.

mcqData.text: Cause of vegetable spoilage—
mcqData.explanation: High heat and moisture (adverse environment) accelerate microbial activity and decay.

mcqData.text: Stem: Kothika uses 2kg sugar for 1kg mango to process and preserve them. Q: What food is processed by Kothika?
mcqData.explanation: Murabba (মোরব্বা) uses a high ratio of sugar syrup to preserve whole or large pieces of fruit.

mcqData.text: What percentage of water is found in fresh fruits and vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: Most fresh produce consists of 80% to 90% water, making them highly perishable.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is used to make sauce in our country?
mcqData.explanation: Tomato sauce/ketchup is the most common processed sauce product in Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: Stem: Kakoly wants to save raw mangoes fallen in a storm. Q: What can she make?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Raw mangoes are ideal for Murabba or long-term pickling.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 31) Q: Preservation methods for fruits: (i) Using extra salt (ii) Using mustard oil (iii) Temp control
mcqData.explanation: Pickling uses salt and oil as natural preservatives to keep fruit edible for years.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) Main reason for preserving fruits/veg?
mcqData.explanation: The biological priority of preservation is preventing decay/rot.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) Main cause of vegetable spoilage?
mcqData.explanation: Respiration continues after harvest, consuming the plant's nutrients and causing rot.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) Water percentage in fresh fruits/veg?
mcqData.explanation: High water content ($>80\%$) is the reason why fruits/veg spoil so quickly.

mcqData.text: For which of the following crops is 'Curing' necessary?
mcqData.explanation: Tuber crops like Sweet Potato require curing to heal skin wounds before storage.

mcqData.text: What is the reason for 'Curing'?
mcqData.explanation: Curing dries the outer skin and heals bruises to prevent pathogens from entering.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) Which gas is emitted most during fruit ripening?
mcqData.explanation: Ethylene is known as the "Ripening Hormone" of the plant kingdom.

mcqData.text: What is the moisture content in dried vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: To prevent spoilage, dried vegetables must have a very low moisture level ($6-8\%$).

mcqData.text: What causes Anthracnose?
mcqData.explanation: Anthracnose is a common fungal disease that creates dark spots on fruits.

mcqData.text: What is released from vegetables as a result of respiration?
mcqData.explanation: Like all living tissues, harvested vegetables consume oxygen and release $CO_2$.

mcqData.text: What is the ideal storage period for Mangoes (at room temp)?
mcqData.explanation: Fresh mangoes typically stay in good condition for 7 to 13 days after ripening.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) What is the moisture content in dried vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: Lowering moisture to 6-8% prevents microbial growth in dried produce.

mcqData.text: At what temperature (°C) do fruits start to rot quickly?
mcqData.explanation: High tropical temperatures (over $30^{\circ}C$) accelerate enzyme activity and decay.

mcqData.text: What is the primary cause of fruit spoilage?
mcqData.explanation: Bacteria and fungi are the direct agents that cause rot and decomposition.

mcqData.text: What happens if the respiration process in fruit is too rapid?
mcqData.explanation: Rapid respiration oxidizes stored sugars/starch, leading to quick weight loss and decay.

mcqData.text: What causes fruit to ripen and soften quickly?
mcqData.explanation: Enzymes like pectinase break down cell walls, making the fruit soft.

mcqData.text: How many grams of vegetables should an adult eat daily?
mcqData.explanation: Nutritionists recommend a daily intake of 220-235g of vegetables for a healthy diet.

mcqData.text: Most significant role in fruit spoilage: (i) Temp (ii) Coldness (iii) Microbes
mcqData.explanation: High temperature and microbes work together to spoil fruit; coldness actually preserves it.

mcqData.text: Fruits/vegetables can be ruined by: (i) Toxins (ii) Internal chemical reactions (iii) Enzymes
mcqData.explanation: All three factors contribute to the loss of quality and edibility of produce.

mcqData.text: Symptoms of fruit spoilage: (i) Foul smell (ii) Injury marks (iii) Discoloration
mcqData.explanation: While injury helps spoilage, foul smell and discoloration are the actual symptoms of rot.

mcqData.text: Causes of fruit spoilage: (i) Respiration (ii) Humidity (iii) Microbes
mcqData.explanation: These are the three main physiological and biological drivers of spoilage.

mcqData.text: Reasons for veg spoilage after harvest: (i) Internal disaster (ii) Reduced respiration (iii) Wounds
mcqData.explanation: Internal physiological breakdown and injuries lead to rot; respiration rate usually increases.

mcqData.text: What amount of citric acid is found in lemon juice?
mcqData.explanation: Lemon juice typically contains about 5% to 6% citric acid.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Ideal storage period for Mangoes?
mcqData.explanation: Most fresh mango varieties last about 1-2 weeks after being picked mature.

mcqData.text: Preserving processed food in bottles or tins is called—
mcqData.explanation: Canning is the process of sealing food in airtight containers and heat-sterilizing them.

mcqData.text: Which gas smoke is used to treat/sterilize vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: Sulfur dioxide ($SO_2$) smoke is used to prevent browning and kill surface microbes.

mcqData.text: Into how many groups can fruit preservation methods be divided?
mcqData.explanation: Generally divided into two: Short-term (Fresh) and Long-term (Processed).

mcqData.text: In what state are fruits preserved with wax coating?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Waxing is used on fresh produce to reduce moisture loss.

mcqData.text: How can vegetables be preserved for a long time?
mcqData.explanation: Cold storage (hima-gar) is the best method for long-term commercial preservation.

mcqData.text: Who is the inventor of the canning (tinning) method?
mcqData.explanation: Nicholas Appert developed the method of heat-processing food in sealed glass jars in 1810.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) Which of the following fruits can be preserved by drying?
mcqData.explanation: Grapes are commercially dried to produce raisins.

mcqData.text: In which process are drying and freezing done simultaneously?
mcqData.explanation: Dehydro-freezing removes some moisture before freezing to maintain texture and save space.

mcqData.text: What should be the concentration of brine (salt water) for fruit preservation?
mcqData.explanation: A 15-20% salt solution is ideal for inhibiting microbial growth in many fruits and vegetables.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) The process of keeping vegetables in boiling water for a short time is called—
mcqData.explanation: Blanching inactivates enzymes that cause spoilage and loss of flavor/color.

mcqData.text: Which acid is commonly used in fruit preservation?
mcqData.explanation: Benzoic acid (and its salts) is a widely used preservative for acidic foods and fruits.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) What is the method of preserving fruits/veg by drying called?
mcqData.explanation: Dehydration removes moisture to a level where bacteria and mold cannot grow.

mcqData.text: What happens when oil is applied to the surface of vegetables?
mcqData.explanation: Oil acts as a barrier to moisture loss and prevents some microbes from entering.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Dipping vegetables in hot water before preservation is—
mcqData.explanation: Blanching is a critical step before freezing or drying vegetables.

mcqData.text: What is the process of sterilizing food by using Gamma rays called?
mcqData.explanation: Food irradiation kills pests and microorganisms without using heat or chemicals.

mcqData.text: What is the system called through which produce reaches the consumer?
mcqData.explanation: Marketing encompasses all steps from harvest to the final sale to the consumer.

mcqData.text: Which is important in fruit marketing?
mcqData.explanation: Grading ensures price transparency by sorting fruits based on size, color, and quality.

mcqData.text: Important factors in vegetable preservation: (i) Light (ii) Ventilation (iii) Relative Humidity
mcqData.explanation: Controlled humidity and air circulation are vital to prevent wilting and mold.

mcqData.text: (Repeat) Marketing problems in BD: (i) Transport (ii) Traffic jams (iii) Extortion
mcqData.explanation: These systemic issues increase the cost and spoilage of perishable goods.

mcqData.text: Fruits that can be preserved by drying: (i) Mango (ii) Jujube (iii) Apple
mcqData.explanation: All three can be sliced and dried (either sun-dried or mechanically) for long-term use.

mcqData.text: What is controlled in cold storage (Himaghar)? (i) High temp (ii) High humidity (iii) Low temp
mcqData.explanation: (Option iii only/ii & iii) Cold storage maintains low temperature and optimum humidity.

mcqData.text: Fruit can be preserved: (i) Fresh (ii) Wet (iii) Processed
mcqData.explanation: Keeping them wet usually speeds up rot; fresh or processed (syrup/jam) is best.

mcqData.text: Grading is very important in vegetables: (i) In marketing (ii) In production (iii) For export
mcqData.explanation: Grading is a post-harvest marketing activity, especially critical for international trade.

mcqData.text: Food is preserved in the canning method: (i) In tin cans (ii) In polybags (iii) In cans
mcqData.explanation: Canning specifically refers to airtight sealing in metal cans or glass jars.

mcqData.text: Used in preserving fruits/veg: (i) Sodium Benzoate (ii) Hydrogen Peroxide (iii) Sulfur Dioxide
mcqData.explanation: Sodium Benzoate and $SO_2$ are safe preservatives; $H_2O_2$ is not used for this.

mcqData.text: Stem: Asha had leftover vegetables after cooking and stored them for the next day. Q: Which storage method did Asha use?
mcqData.explanation: Storing vegetables for just one or two days is considered a short-term preservation method.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 81) Q: Asha can store vegetables: (i) In the coolest part of the house (ii) In a heap (iii) In a dark place
mcqData.explanation: Vegetables should never be kept in a heap (stupa) as it generates heat and speeds up rot.

mcqData.text: Stem: Rashid grew potatoes; to get a better price later, he used a long-term storage method. Q: Which method did he use?
mcqData.explanation: Cold storage is the standard industrial method for preserving potatoes for months.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 83) Q: Due to Rashid's decision: (i) Crop is saved from rot (ii) Year-round demand met (iii) Fair price obtained
mcqData.explanation: Industrial storage helps stabilize market prices and ensures a steady food supply.

mcqData.text: Stem: Shipra uses an acidic liquid to preserve food. Q: Which substance does Shipra use?
mcqData.explanation: Vinegar (Acetic acid) is a safe and common household acidic preservative.

mcqData.text: (Correction) Which processes use chemical preservatives?
mcqData.explanation: While both use them, pickles (আচার) primarily rely on acids/salts for preservation.

mcqData.text: Stem: Alim's mangoes rotted last year in a closed room. This year he stored them properly. Q: Which method did he use this year?
mcqData.explanation: Proper natural storage involves good ventilation and shade, preventing heat build-up.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 87) Q: Why did the mangoes rot last year? (i) Microbial attack (ii) Uncontrolled temp/humidity (iii) Storage errors
mcqData.explanation: A closed room increases heat and humidity, which triggers both microbes and decay.

mcqData.text: Which substance helps Jelly to set/solidify?
mcqData.explanation: Pectin is a natural fiber in fruits that creates the gelatinous texture of jelly.

mcqData.text: Which element plays the key role in solidifying fruit juice with sugar?
mcqData.explanation: Pectin reacts with sugar and acid to form the "mesh" that sets the jam/jelly.

mcqData.text: Which acid is added during the preparation of Jelly?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Citric acid (often from lemon) is essential for the pectin to set correctly.

mcqData.text: Citric acid in 100g lemon juice equals how many grams of pure citric acid?
mcqData.explanation: On average, lemon juice contains about 5 grams of citric acid per 100ml/100g.

mcqData.text: Which part of the fruit is used for making Jam?
mcqData.explanation: Jams are made from fruit pulp (crushed fruit), whereas jellies use filtered juice.

mcqData.text: Generally, what percentage of fruit juice is in Jelly?
mcqData.explanation: Standard fruit jelly must contain at least 45% fruit juice by weight.

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) Which vegetable is used to make French fries?
mcqData.explanation: Potato is the universal choice for French fries.

mcqData.text: Which fruit can be preserved by applying heat (processing)?
mcqData.explanation: Mangoes are heat-processed into pulps, jams, and murabbas for long-term use.

mcqData.text: Used in making Pickles: (i) Citric acid (ii) Vinegar (iii) Salt
mcqData.explanation: (Option ii & iii) Salt and Vinegar (Acetic acid) are the primary agents for pickling.

mcqData.text: Which of the following are used in making Jelly? (i) Sulfuric acid (ii) Malic acid (iii) Citric acid
mcqData.explanation: Citric and Malic acids are natural fruit acids that help pectin to set. Sulfuric acid is toxic.

mcqData.text: Murabba (sweet preserve) can be made from: (i) Mango (ii) Bottle Gourd (iii) White Gourd
mcqData.explanation: Mango, Bottle Gourd (Lau), and White Gourd (Chalkumra) are the most common bases for Murabba.

mcqData.text: Adding Citric acid to Jelly: (i) Releases pectin (ii) Increases shelf life (iii) Enhances taste
mcqData.explanation: Acid helps extract pectin from fruit tissues, acts as a preservative, and balances sweetness.

mcqData.text: For which fruits is a dense sugar solution directly used to make Murabba? (i) Watermelon (ii) Mango (iii) Pineapple
mcqData.explanation: (Option ii & iii) Mango and Pineapple are directly simmered in heavy syrup for Murabba.

mcqData.text: Stem: Zubaida preserves fruits using a sugar solution of at least 60% concentration. Q: What type of preservation is this?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) High-sugar processing (osmotic dehydration) inhibits microbial growth.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 105) Q: Which fruits can be preserved this way? (i) Mango (ii) Guava (iii) Pineapple
mcqData.explanation: These fleshy fruits absorb sugar syrup well, making them ideal for long-term preservation.

mcqData.text: Stem: Jamila uses an acidic substance to process and preserve fruits/veg. Q: Which substance does Jamila use?
mcqData.explanation: Vinegar is the common commercial/household term for the acidic preservative used.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 107) Q: Where is this acidic preservative used? (i) Pickles (ii) Sauce (iii) Chutney
mcqData.explanation: Vinegar is a key ingredient in almost all processed acidic condiments like pickles and sauces.

mcqData.text: Stem: Kothika uses 2kg sugar for 1kg mango to preserve them for a long time. Q: What food is Kothika making?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) The high sugar-to-fruit ratio is the hallmark of making Murabba (মোরব্বা).

mcqData.text: (Repeat) Cause of vegetable spoilage—
mcqData.explanation: High heat and humidity (adverse environment) are the leading triggers for rot.

mcqData.text: Stem: Kakoly wants to save storm-fallen raw mangoes. Q: What can she make?
mcqData.explanation: (Repeat) Murabba is a classic way to utilize raw mangoes before they spoil.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 111) Q: Kakoly's preservation method involves: (i) Extra salt (ii) Mustard oil (iii) Temp control
mcqData.explanation: Pickling raw mangoes requires salt and oil; room temperature is usually sufficient for pickles.

mcqData.text: Stem: Mili's mother told her to add a specific component to preserve mango chutney for a long time. Q: What did she suggest?
mcqData.explanation: Sodium Benzoate is a powerful chemical preservative used for long-term commercial storage.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 113) Q: What else could Mili make with raw mangoes? (i) Murabba (ii) Sauce (iii) Jelly
mcqData.explanation: (Option i & iii) Raw mango is perfect for Murabba and Jelly; Sauce is usually made from ripe tomatoes.

mcqData.text: Stem: Sathi used lemon juice to help her mango jelly set. Q: What could she use instead of lemon juice?
mcqData.explanation: Pure Citric acid powder provides the same chemical reaction as lemon juice for setting jelly.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Same as Q. 115) Q: Lemon juice in Sathi's jelly: (i) Extracts pectin (ii) Improves preservation (iii) Enhances taste
mcqData.explanation: Acid is multi-functional: it helps the structure, prevents spoilage, and adds tanginess.

mcqData.text: Stem: Ruma was making jelly and used lemon juice as a setting agent. Q: What other component could Ruma have used?
mcqData.explanation: Pectin is the actual substance responsible for the "gel" in jelly; lemon juice just helps it work.
